channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Early voting begins in New York City mayor's race and contest to pick New Jersey's next governor
item.description: Polling places open Saturday for in-person voting in two of the year’s most closely watched elections: The New York City mayor’s race and the contest to pick New Jersey’s next governor.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dallas pediatrician sued over providing hormone treatments to teens surrenders medical license
item.description: After Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton sued last year, Dr. May Lau said she is moving to Oregon and doesn't need a Texas license anymore.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Xiomara Moore, KPRC 2

item.title: DeSantis likely to sign bill allowing Ivermectin over the counter
item.description: In a social media post, Gov. Ron DeSantis said he’s pushing the legislature to pass a bill involving Ivermectin and he plans to sign it.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration won't tap contingency fund to keep food aid flowing, memo says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is rejecting the idea of using roughly $5 billion in contingency funds to keep food aid flowing into November amid the government shutdown, according to a Department of Agriculture memo that surfaced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reagan Foundation becomes the latest US institution drawn into Donald Trump's controversies
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation and Institute is drawing blowback and praise for arguing that a Canadian government advertisement playing in U.S. markets misrepresented the 40th president’s words to blast President Donald Trump’s tariff policies.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow And Thalia Beaty, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New Jersey early voting 2025: When locations open and how to find them
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) -- While Election Day is fast approaching, New Jersey residents will be able to cast their ballot as early as this weekend. In 2021, New Jersey established in-person early voting for registered voters at General Election polling locations.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, PIX 11

item.title: Politics Friday Special: Rochester's growth spurt is driving economic, political change
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst hosts a special broadcast of Politics Friday from Rochester, Minn., exploring how the city’s makeover plays into the regional hub’s economic and political standing.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Mystery Trump ally donates $130M to cover troops’ paychecks amid shutdown chaos
item.description: An anonymous donor gave $130 million to the Department of War to pay U.S. troops during the government shutdown, but legal restrictions may require congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnson shuts down House to pressure Schumer as government standstill nears one month
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson canceled legislative session for another week in a bid to pressure Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer amid the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inflation data unlikely to be released next month, White House says
item.description: The White House says there probably won't be an October inflation report due to the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and Kim Jong Un should make 'bold decision' to meet during his Asia trip, South Korean official says
item.description: South Korea's unification minister Chung Dong-young urges President Donald Trump and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un to meet during Trump's trip to Asia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security recipients get a 2.8% cost-of-living boost in 2026, average of $56 per month
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration's annual cost-of-living adjustment will go up by 2.8% in 2026, translating to an average increase of more than $56 for retirees every month, agency officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As strikes on alleged drug boats grow, so do questions about their legality and goal
item.description: President Donald Trump says he has authority to carry out the strikes, but international experts are asking if the attacks are truly about countering narcotics or instead toppling Venezuela's Nicolás Maduro.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez and Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Transportation chief warns of travel impacts amid shutdown
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy has said the federal safety system depends on air traffic controllers working without pay.
item.dc:creator: Meg Hilling, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats see Hegseth curtailing Congress's oversight role
item.description: Some Democrats are accusing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth of curtailing Congress’s oversight role with his recent order instructing Defense Department personnel to coordinate all interactions with Capitol Hill through the building’s central legislative affairs office.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration uses shutdown to push Education Department elimination
item.description: The Education Department is trying to use the government shutdown to further push for its own elimination.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: NY AG Letitia James to make first court appearance in mortgage fraud case
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James is set to make her first court appearance in a mortgage fraud case on Friday, the third adversary of President Donald Trump to face a judge on federal charges in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In Japan and South Korea, Trump will promote big investments. But the details are still not clear
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is going to Japan and South Korea next week to promote an epic financial windfall — at least $900 billion in investments for U.S. factories, a natural gas pipeline and other projects.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeSantis defends law allowing charter schools on public school campuses
item.description: Over the summer, lawmakers gave operators the ability to use vacant areas on any public-school campus with low enrollment.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump gives disaster declarations to Alaska and others but denies Illinois, Vermont and Maryland
item.description: President Donald Trump approved major disaster declarations for Alaska, Nebraska, North Dakota and the Leech Lake Band of Ojibwe late Wednesday, while denying requests from Vermont, Illinois and Maryland and leaving other states still waiting for answers.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Florida teenager is in an Israeli prison. Why his family and advocates say he should be released
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — A Florida family already grieving the beating death of a 20-year-old relative is now pleading with U.S. leaders to help free the dead man’s cousin, a 16-year-old Palestinian-American from Florida who has been held in an Israeli prison for eight months.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, JULIA FRANKEL and JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poll: Texans split on Trump's immigration policies
item.description: A new poll released Wednesday finds registered voters in Texas are split on immigration policies implemented under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: California leaders write letter to Hegseth, demand answers for artillery fire over I-5
item.description: CAMP PENDLETON, Calif. (FOX 5/KUSI) -- California Democrats sent a letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Wednesday regarding shrapnel that struck California Highway Patrol (CHP) vehicles during Vice President JD Vance’s visit to Camp Pendleton.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Wyoming Capitol evacuated after report of suspected explosive device
item.description: The Wyoming Capitol in Cheyenne was evacuated on Tuesday after a report about a suspected explosive device, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: George Santos says he's 'looking into' repaying fraud victims
item.description: Santos was ordered to pay more than $370,000 in restitution, but the president's clemency order wiped the debt clean.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Preservation group urges pause in White House East Wing demolition
item.description: The destruction of a portion of the White House’s East Wing to create the ballroom has hit a nerve among critics.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Florida bill seeks to label political offenses as hate crimes
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) - In the wake of recent politically motivated attacks across the nation, one state lawmaker is looking to change Florida statute. A new bill at the capitol is looking to expand Florida's hate-crime statute, shifting how hate crimes are defined and punished.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USDA reopening 2,100 aid offices despite shutdown
item.description: The Agriculture Department will reopen about 2,100 county offices all across the country Thursday despite the ongoing government shutdown to help farmers and ranchers get access to $3 billion of aid from existing programs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tribes struggle as shutdown continues
item.description: The United States is 20 days into the third-longest federal government shutdown in history and Indian Country is bracing for an unstable future ahead as reserve funding for many tribes could begin to dry up.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: DeSantis joins Democrat in nationwide push for term limits in Congress
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Governor Ron DeSantis is teaming up with a former Democratic representative to make a push for term limits in Congress. On Tuesday, the governor announced he and former Rep. David Trone of Maryland would co-chair U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Arrests after protesters stage sit-in at state building
item.description: Protesters staged a sit-in Tuesday in the lobby area of the Retirement Systems Building in St. Paul, calling on the state to cut financial ties to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: What the federal government shutdown means countrywide
item.description: The federal government remains shut down. The NPR Network is following the ways the shutdown is affecting services across the country.
item.dc:creator: The NPR Network, MPR News

item.title: White House starts demolishing part of the East Wing
item.description: Dramatic photos show construction equipment tearing into the East Wing façade and windows, though the federal agency that oversees such projects has not approved President Trump's 90,000-square-foot, $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: China hits the U.S. back in trade war
item.description: China has expanded its export rules on rare earths, requiring foreign firms to get approval for exporting products containing even small amounts of China-originated materials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
